2605.03842.title: SOAR: Real-Time Joint Optimization of Order Allocation and Robot Scheduling in Robotic Mobile Fulfillment Systems
2605.03842.paper_summary: Robotic Mobile Fulfillment Systems (RMFS) rely on mobile robots for automated inventory transportation, coordinating order allocation and robot scheduling to enhance warehousing efficiency. However, optimizing RMFS is challenging due to strict real-time constraints and the strong coupling of multi-phase decisions. Existing methods either decompose the problem into isolated sub-tasks to guarantee responsiveness at the cost of global optimality, or rely on computationally expensive global optimization models that are unsuitable for dynamic industrial environments. To bridge this gap, we propose SOAR, a unified Deep Reinforcement Learning framework for real-time joint optimization. SOAR transforms order allocation and robot scheduling into a unified process by utilizing soft order allocations as observations. We formulate this as an Event-Driven Markov Decision Process, enabling the agent to perform simultaneous scheduling in response to asynchronous system events. Technically, we employ a Heterogeneous Graph Transformer to encode the warehouse state and integrate phased domain knowledge. Additionally, we incorporate a reward shaping strategy to address sparse feedback in long-horizon tasks. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world industrial datasets, in collaboration with Geekplus, demonstrate that SOAR reduces global makespan by 7.5\% and average order completion time by 15.4\% with sub-100ms latency. Furthermore, sim-to-real deployment confirms its practical viability and significant performance gains in production environments. The code is available at https://github.com/200815147/SOAR.
2605.03842.paper_summary_zh: 機器人移動履行系統（RMFS）依賴移動機器人進行自動化庫存運輸，協調訂單分配和機器人排程以提高倉儲效率。<lb/>然而，由於嚴格的實時限制和多階段決策的強耦合，優化RMFS面臨挑戰。<lb/>現有方法要麼將問題分解為孤立的子任務，以保證響應速度，但以全球最優性為代價，要麼依賴計算成本高昂的全局優化模型，這些模型不適合動態工業環境。<lb/>為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了SOAR，一個統一的深度增強學習框架，用於實時聯合優化。<lb/>SOAR通過利用軟性訂單分配作為觀察，將訂單分配和機器人排程轉變為一個統一的過程。<lb/>我們將其表述為事件驅動的馬爾可夫決策過程，使代理能夠對異步系統事件進行同時排程。<lb/>在技術上，我們使用異質圖Transformer來編碼倉庫狀態並整合分階段的領域知識。<lb/>此外，我們還納入了一種獎勵塑形策略，以解決長期任務中稀疏反饋的問題。<lb/>與Geekplus合作的合成和真實工業數據集上的大量實驗表明，SOAR將全球完成時間縮短了7.5\%，平均訂單完成時間縮短了15.4\%，延遲低於100毫秒。<lb/>此外，模擬到現實的部署確認了其在生產環境中的實際可行性和顯著的性能提升。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/200815147/SOAR 獲得。
2605.03842.author: Yibang Tang et.al.
2605.03842.authors: Yibang Tang, Yifan Yang, Jingyuan Wang, Junhua Chen, Zhen Zhao
